mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions that drive corporate profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 3.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 2%+ year-over-year increase in CPI inflation accelerates Fed tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2%, or the regression coefficient for CPIAUCSL turns positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO < 12mo MA, or the signal's predictive power drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) and stagnant wage inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates corporate cost-management effectiveness, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-CPIAUCSL divergence fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising consumer confidence (RSAFS) indicates a demand-side recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to produce negative 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.1 in rolling window tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 6-month trend combined with rising commodity prices (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-CPIAUCSL divergence fails to produce negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.1).
